title: The Grand Opening

jokes.text: That's the adult equivalent of 'The dog ate my homework.'
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggests maybe Susie really had a dentist appointment
jokes.punchline: Larry compares adult excuse-making to classic childhood lie
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor with good economy. The parallel between adult and child excuse-making is clever and well-delivered.

jokes.text: Can't you just say yes? / No.
jokes.setup: Larry asks Jeff to do a favor, Jeff says maybe
jokes.punchline: Jeff flatly refuses to commit to automatic agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for both Larry and Jeff - Larry's expectation of automatic compliance vs Jeff's reasonable boundaries.

jokes.text: What does that have to do with a cough? I don't understand that.
jokes.setup: Osteopath recommends colon cleanse for Larry's cough
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical confusion about the connection
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic - questioning dubious medical advice with genuine confusion rather than skepticism.

jokes.text: Asshole.
jokes.setup: Andy Portico gives brutal restaurant review on TV
jokes.punchline: Larry's simple, direct assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word that captures Larry's feeling about the critic. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: It's real original, the thumbs down.
jokes.setup: Jeff comments on Portico's 'clever' thumbs down gesture
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic dismissal of the unoriginal gimmick
jokes.explanation: Larry's disdain for clichéd critic gimmicks, delivered with perfect sarcasm.

jokes.text: I couldn't get dates. You don't see me taking it out on anybody.
jokes.setup: Larry suggests critic takes out dating frustrations on restaurants
jokes.punchline: Larry claims he doesn't take it out on anyone (obviously untrue)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-deception - Larry's complete lack of self-awareness about his own behavior patterns. This is peak Larry David character comedy.

jokes.text: I don't know about that.
jokes.setup: Larry claims he doesn't take frustrations out on people
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's understated disagreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-woman response - Cheryl's gentle but pointed contradiction of Larry's delusion.

jokes.text: You know how bookstores make you feel stupid? Health food stores make me feel unhealthy.
jokes.setup: Comparison setup about how certain stores make you feel
jokes.punchline: Unexpected parallel between bookstores/stupid and health stores/unhealthy
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational comedy with perfect parallel structure. The unexpected comparison is both relatable and original.

jokes.text: It's for my wife. It's for my wife.
jokes.setup: Larry asks health store employee about colon cleanser
jokes.punchline: Larry's embarrassed over-explanation that it's not for him
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry discomfort moment - his need to clarify creates more awkwardness than necessary.

jokes.text: That's the chef. / Our chef's bald.
jokes.setup: They recognize someone who looks like their chef
jokes.punchline: Jeff's confusion because their chef is supposed to be bald
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the toupée reveal - the contradiction creates perfect comedic tension.

jokes.text: I just assumed... and I was right... that you were one of those people who find that men who enhance their appearance are wrong and stupid so I took my toupee off.
jokes.setup: Larry confronts the chef about his appearance change
jokes.punchline: Chef's elaborate justification for deceptive interviewing strategy
jokes.explanation: Hilariously convoluted logic for deception - the chef's assumption about Larry's prejudices and his strategic response.

jokes.text: You came in bald just to get the job?
jokes.setup: Chef explains his deceptive strategy
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous summary of the absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Larry's disbelief perfectly captures the audience's reaction to this bizarre deception.

jokes.text: For the next five years was he gonna come in every day and leave the toupee at home? / Yeah, right. Or just wear it on weekends? Live a double life.
jokes.setup: Larry questions the chef's long-term deception plan
jokes.punchline: Jeff adds to the absurdity with weekend-only toupee wearing
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the absurdity - imagining the chef's elaborate double life around hair deception.

jokes.text: You would've been all over that fat guy. You would've loved that fat guy, don't tell me.
jokes.setup: Jeff accuses Larry of having hiring preferences for bald people
jokes.punchline: Jeff's aggressive insistence about Larry's supposed fat guy preference
jokes.explanation: Jeff's passionate conviction about Larry's supposed preferences is both absurd and character-revealing.

jokes.text: You have a bald accountant, a bald urologist, a bald travel agent. You have far more bald professionals than the average person.
jokes.setup: Larry denies having a preference for bald people
jokes.punchline: Jeff lists Larry's surprisingly comprehensive collection of bald professionals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity - the detailed list makes Jeff's point undeniable and hilariously specific.

jokes.text: I knew you'd say 'duly noted,' too.
jokes.setup: Larry says 'duly noted' in response
jokes.punchline: Jeff claims he predicted Larry's exact phrase
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Larry's predictable verbal patterns - Jeff knows Larry so well he can predict his phrases.

jokes.text: Poor kid lost his hair, he's getting chemo. / No, he's not going through chemotherapy. Another kid in class is, and a lot of the boys in the senior class as a show of solidarity shaved their heads.
jokes.setup: Larry assumes bald kid has cancer
jokes.punchline: Jeff reveals it's actually a solidarity gesture for another student
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - Larry's assumption about cancer is corrected with heartwarming reality, setting up his next comment.

jokes.text: Maybe one day I'll get a chance to do something good for somebody like that. That's really touching. / You've already got kind of a head start, actually.
jokes.setup: Larry expresses desire to do something good like the solidarity gesture
jokes.punchline: Jeff's pun about Larry having a 'head start' (being bald)
jokes.explanation: Solid pun that works on multiple levels - Jeff pointing out Larry's natural qualification for solidarity.

jokes.text: All right, Larry David. I'm coming to your restaurant next week. / Oh, okay. / I'm gonna review it.
jokes.setup: Portico threatens to review Larry's restaurant while playing dodge ball
jokes.punchline: The awkwardness of being threatened during a children's game
jokes.explanation: Sets up the impending disaster perfectly - Portico's aggressive threat in an innocent setting.

jokes.text: It hit the ground. / Bullshit! / Hit the ground! / Get out of there! / Hit the ground, Portico. You're out!
jokes.setup: Dodge ball dispute over whether ball hit ground first
jokes.punchline: Adult argument escalating over children's game rules
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe escalation - adults getting way too serious about a children's game.

jokes.text: Ow, fuck! What have you done? / What the fuck?
jokes.setup: Larry throws dodge ball aggressively at Portico
jokes.punchline: Portico's injury and outrage from children's game
jokes.explanation: Payoff of escalating tension - Larry's competitive nature causes literal damage to his restaurant's fate.

jokes.text: He's a bald-faced... literally... a bald-faced liar.
jokes.setup: Larry explains firing the chef for deception
jokes.punchline: Larry catches himself making an inadvertent pun about baldness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant accidental pun that Larry recognizes and emphasizes. Perfect callback to the toupée situation.

jokes.text: What about the muck, are we in any muck? / We are in a big muck. / If we're in mire, I would assume that we're also in muck. Usually one is in muck and mire. He said I was in a mire. I was trying to ascertain whether or not muck was involved as well.
jokes.setup: Jeff says they're in a mire
jokes.punchline: Larry's pedantic analysis of muck vs mire terminology
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry pedantry - his obsession with precise language even in crisis situations. The escalating explanation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Like a fucking Muppet.
jokes.setup: Portico can't shake hands due to broken thumbs
jokes.punchline: His self-description as a Muppet with useless hands
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual comparison - the image of Portico with useless hands like a Muppet is both accurate and hilarious.

jokes.text: Because I can't do 'thumbs up, thumbs down.' That's my whole gimmick.
jokes.setup: Portico can't review the restaurant due to injury
jokes.punchline: His entire career depends on thumb gestures he can't perform
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic justice - the gimmick Larry mocked earlier becomes Portico's undoing.

jokes.text: You can't go like this? / I can't go like this.
jokes.setup: Larry asks if Portico can still do thumbs up
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of Portico's inability to perform basic gesture
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy - the absurdity of a critic unable to perform his signature move.

jokes.text: How am I supposed to eat that? Suck it up my ass?
jokes.setup: Assistant brings Portico spaghetti despite his hand injury
jokes.punchline: Portico's crude and angry response about eating difficulty
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective - Portico's frustration expressed in characteristically inappropriate language.

jokes.text: Larry accidentally spilling food on Portico
jokes.setup: Larry tries to help feed Portico
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of Larry making things worse while trying to help
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - his attempt to be helpful only makes the situation worse.

jokes.text: Salmon's out. / Plenty of fish in the sea. / Who needs it?
jokes.setup: New chef Guy refuses to cook salmon
jokes.punchline: Jeff's inadvertent fish pun, Larry's agreement
jokes.explanation: Mild pun that works in context - Jeff's cliché becomes accidentally relevant to fish discussion.

jokes.text: Shit, shitface fuck, shit, cocksucker.
jokes.setup: Guy finishes his tour of the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Sudden explosion of profanity from the chef
jokes.explanation: First reveal of Guy's Tourette's - shocking transition from pleasant conversation to profanity.

jokes.text: Look at your car. Why is your car always so filthy?
jokes.setup: Cheryl notices Larry's dirty car
jokes.punchline: Her disgust at Larry's consistently poor car maintenance
jokes.explanation: Character-based humor about Larry's slovenly habits and Cheryl's standards.

jokes.text: Listen to how low my voice is today. / Oh, hey... down, down, down-nn.
jokes.setup: Cheryl mentions her voice being low
jokes.punchline: Her demonstration of the unnaturally deep voice
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy from the colon cleanse side effects - Cheryl's voice change is unexpected.

jokes.text: Car wash entrapment sequence
jokes.setup: They're stuck in an automated car wash
jokes.punchline: Extended sequence of panic and desperation
jokes.explanation: Extended physical comedy sequence building to Cheryl's bathroom emergency - well-constructed escalation.

jokes.text: I got to go. I got to go! I got to go!
jokes.setup: Cheryl's bathroom emergency during car wash entrapment
jokes.punchline: Her escalating desperation and ultimate defeat
jokes.explanation: Payoff of the colon cleanse setup - embarrassing but earned through proper setup.

jokes.text: A pit plate? Why don't you call it a garbage plate?
jokes.setup: Jeff suggests a pit plate for olives
jokes.punchline: Guy's blunt critique of euphemistic restaurant terminology
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about restaurant pretension - Guy's directness cuts through the BS.

jokes.text: The personality on this guy, huh? He's a piece of work.
jokes.setup: Chef's strong opinions about menu items
jokes.punchline: Larry's assessment of the chef's difficult personality
jokes.explanation: Foreshadowing bigger problems with the demanding chef

jokes.text: Motherfucking cocksucker asshole!
jokes.setup: Guy continues working normally
jokes.punchline: Another sudden outburst of profanity
jokes.explanation: Reinforces Guy's Tourette's - building the pattern for the opening night disaster.

jokes.text: Portico. / Fucking Portico! That prick, I don't believe it. He's laughing right now. That motherfucker.
jokes.setup: They realize Portico recommended Guy knowing about his Tourette's
jokes.punchline: Larry's realization that Portico set them up
jokes.explanation: Brilliant realization moment - Larry understanding he's been played perfectly. Great callback structure.

jokes.text: He's a survivor.
jokes.setup: Larry wants to fire Guy for his Tourette's
jokes.punchline: Jeff's simple explanation for why they can't
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and dark humor - two words that explain the impossible situation they're in.

jokes.text: Why is he cursing in English? 'Mutherfuck-erre.' / Where did he get that from? / 'Cocksuck-erre.'
jokes.setup: Larry wonders why French chef curses in English
jokes.punchline: Larry's imitation of French-accented English profanity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational question with perfect delivery - Larry's accent work makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Maybe one day, I'll get a chance to do something good for somebody like that.
jokes.setup: Callback to Larry's earlier comment about helping
jokes.punchline: Ironic juxtaposition with the chaos he's created
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure - Larry's earnest earlier comment becomes deeply ironic given the disaster.

jokes.text: Fuck you, you car wash cunt! / I had a dental appointment!
jokes.setup: Guy's profanity continues
jokes.punchline: Susie's defensive response about her canceled lunch becomes part of the chaos
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback integration - multiple storylines colliding in the chaos of Guy's outburst.

tmdb_overview: Larry figures out a way to fire a chef, and alienate an important restaurant critic, in the days before his new restaurant is scheduled to open. As Susie stews over a misunderstanding with Larry and Cheryl, the Davids spend some quality time at the car wash. With its grand opening at hand, the restaurant owners hire a new chef with a penchant for speaking his mind.
